claims.evidence_text: Canopy cover estimation using Canopeo app for (a) planophile variety showing 82% cover, and (b) erectophile variety showing 65% cover.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat canopy cover 82% (planophile) vs 65% (erectophile) variety; planophiles close canopy earlier.

claims.evidence_text: Trials had two planting dates (late Sept. and late Oct.), two seeding rates (1.98 and 4.94 million seeds-1 ha) ... Seeding rates did not impact yield
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat seeding rates of 1.98 and 4.94 million seeds/ha tested; rate did not impact yield. NOTE: unit is million seeds/ha.

claims.evidence_text: erectophile varieties had greater RUE under early planting while also achieving high radiation interception ... beneficial under higher yield environments (e.g., early planting, narrow rows, intensive management)
claims.claim_summary: Erectophile winter wheat varieties show greater radiation use efficiency under early planting/high-yield environments.

claims.evidence_text: Early planting date significantly improved yield in all growing seasons ... Timely planting was most critical for higher yields in this research while lowering seeding rates did not impact yield.
claims.claim_summary: Early/timely planting (late Sept vs late Oct) significantly improved winter wheat yield across seasons in Michigan.
